---
title: "Publications"
---
I position my research at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and well-being, examining how AI reshapes consumer behavior, ethical perceptions, and sustainability across marketing, tourism, and management.

:::{#tab-fund}

| No. | Paper | Status | Journal ranking |
|---|---------------|------|---------------|
| 1 | **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, S. (2025). Financial self-discrepancy, consumer well-being, and luxury preferences: The moderating effect of access-based luxury formats. *Psychology & Marketing*, 43, 357–373. [https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057](https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057) | Published | Q1, IF = 9.1, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 2 | **Cao, T. T.**, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Conflicted by faith, drawn to pleasure: How religious self-discrepancy shapes product choices. *International Journal of Consumer Studies*, 50(2), e70202. [https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202) | Published | Q1, IF = 7.7, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 3 | **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup>, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2025). Compensatory consumption: A review and research agenda using the theory-context-characteristics-methodology framework. *International Journal of Consumer Studies*, 49(5), e701112. [https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112)  | Published       | Q1, IF = 7.7, <br> A-ranked ABDC      |
| 4 | **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2025). Self-discrepancy and gambling retention: A conceptual model of motivational and moral pathways. *Strategic Business Research*, 1(1), 100001. [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001) | Published | Peer-reviewed journal |
| 5 | **Cao, T. T.**, & Nguyen, S.<sup>c</sup> (2024). Navigating dual identities: How bicultural consumers leverage luxury consumption to cope with perceived discrimination and enhance well-being. *Journal of Development and Integration*, 85, 106–116. [https://doi.org/10.61602/jdi.2025.85.12](https://doi.org/10.61602/jdi.2025.85.12) | Published       | Peer-reviewed journal                 |
| 6 | Ha, N. M., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2019). Constructing athlete identification and the effectiveness of athlete endorsement on customer's purchase intention. *Journal of Distribution Science*, 17(8). [https://doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87](https://doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87) | Published | Peer-reviewed journal |
| 7 | Mai, N., **Cao, T.**, & Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup> (2025). How hotels communicate sustainability goal revisions: The role of perceived green-rinsing, influencer type, and sustainability solutions. *Journal of Sustainable Tourism*. | First Revision | Q1, IF = 9.9, <br> A*-ranked ABDC |
| 8 | **Cao, T. T.**, Yunen, Z., & Tuan, N.<sup>c</sup> (2026). When Generative Artificial Intelligence becomes a counsellor: How employee evaluations shape engagement and organizational attractiveness. *Journal of Enterprise Information Management*. | Second Revision | Q1, IF = 7.6, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 9 | **Cao, T. T.**, & Tuan, N.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Understanding consumer purchase intention toward Zero-Click GenAI-generated recommendations: Evidence from structural equation modeling and machine learning. *Journal of Internet Commerce*. | Second Revision | Q1, IF = 4.3, <br> B-ranked ABDC |
| 10 | **Cao, T. T.**, Mai, N., Tuan, N.<sup>c</sup>, Malik, A., & Anjali, T. (2026). How employees evaluate Artificial Intelligence-generated leaders: Perceived responsibility, ethicality, and authenticity as pathways to compliance. *The Service Industries Journal*. | First Revision | Q1, IF = 7.4, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 11 | Tuan, N., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2026). Working with GenAI: How human-centered mindset and GenAI understanding drive employee autonomy and competence. *Journal of Enterprise Information Management*. | Submission | Q1, IF = 7.6, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 12 | Mai, N., **Cao, T.**, Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup>, & Tingwen X. (2025). Deepfake versus Virtual Influencers: How Influencer Diversity and Corporate Social Responsibility Shape Consumer Ethicality and Engagement. *Journal of Business Ethics*. | Under Review | Q1, IF = 8.7, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 13 | Yunen, Z., Mai, N., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2026). When synthetic endorsers are morally acceptable: Context-dependent consumer responses to AI-generated synthetic endorsers. *Journal of Business Ethics*. | Submission | Q1, IF = 8.7, <br> A-ranked ABDC |
| 14 | **Cao, T. T.**, Mai, N., & Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup> (2026). Deepfake versus human influencers in hospitality marketing: The roles of emotional exhaustion, autonomy, and identity similarity in shaping booking intention. *Journal of Hospitality and Tourism Management*. | Submission | Q1, IF = 8.7, <br> A*-ranked ABDC |
| 15 | Yunen, Z., **Cao, T.**, & Mai, N.<sup>c</sup> (2025). The right endorser for the right stay: How human and AI endorsers differ across privacy levels and service modes in accommodation booking intention. *Journal of Travel Research*. | Under Review | Q1, IF = 9.8, <br> A*-ranked ABDC |
| 16 | Nguyen, M., **Cao, T. T.**, Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup>, & Yang, B. (2026). From Virtual Endorsers to Booking Intention: Understanding Consumer Responses to AI-Generated Lodging Advertising. *Tourism Management*. | Submission | Q1, IF = 9.9, <br> A*-ranked ABDC |
| 17 | **Cao, T. T.**, Mai, N., & Yunen, Z.<sup>c</sup> (2025). How deepfake influencers shape hotel booking decisions. *Journal of Travel Research*.| Submission | Q1 JCR, IF = 9.8, <br> A*-ranked ABDC |
| 18 | Huong, P., Thinh, D., & **Cao, T. T.**<sup>c</sup> (2025). Revaluing waste: The roles of true-to-ideal authenticity, actual-ought discrepancy, and user-generated content in willingness to pay for upcycled food. *International Journal of Consumer Studies*. | Submission | Q1 JCR, IF = 7.7, <br> A-ranked ABDC |

:::
